text: OK, I'll send you a payment to give me a couple days because right now. It's the Fourth of July holiday weekend. I can't do anything until Monday.
focus: Payment direction, who needs extra time, and the US holiday

text: The account that I'm paying you from is the same account I'm receiving from Amazon, so sometimes there's money in that account and sometimes I have to transfer.
focus: Who pays whom and where the money comes from

text: I am set up to send for Jessica, but she's going to need to transfer the documents.
focus: Resolve she to Jessica without inventing a relationship

text: Don't submit the VAT return until Peter confirms the figures.
focus: Negation and action ordering

text: If the refund doesn't arrive by Friday, pay the supplier from the backup account.
focus: Condition, deadline, and payment source

text: Please invoice EUR 12,450.75, not EUR 12,540.75, under PO-8841.
focus: Exact numbers, negation, and identifier

text: Send the draft to ops@example.com and upload it at https://example.com/a?x=1.
focus: Exact email and URL

text: I've sent the revised contract, so the ball is in their court now.
focus: Natural idiom rather than literal ball/court wording

text: Let's circle back after the client has slept on it.
focus: Two business idioms

text: I'm gonna head out. Ping me if anything blows up.
focus: Informal tone and nonliteral emergency wording

context: [Lan] I will ask Minh about the invoice.
text: She said he already paid it.
focus: Use context for female/male referents without adding facts

context: [Anh Nam] Em gửi báo giá chưa?
text: Gửi rồi anh nhé.
focus: Recover first-person subject and respectful tone

text: Anh gửi em file nhé.
focus: Workplace pronouns must not become literal siblings

context: [Linh] Chị Hoa đang giữ hợp đồng.
text: Nhờ chị ký giúp em với ạ.
focus: Polite request and contextual female addressee

context: [Chris] I only have the old copy.
text: Ask him to send it to me before lunch.
focus: Resolve him to Ben and me to the current speaker

context: [Anna] Ben and Chris both reviewed it.
text: He said it was fine.
focus: Do not invent which man when context is ambiguous

text: Để mình gọi cho.
focus: Own-message first person and volunteered action

text: That one left yesterday.
focus: Resolve that one to the red shipment

context: [Tôi] Hóa đơn trong ảnh bị mờ.
text: Để mình chụp lại nhé.
focus: Infer retaking the photo without translating media placeholder

text: Để tôi nói lại.
focus: Natural response to an unclear voice message

text:
TOTAL DUE: USD 1,280.00
PAY BY: 07/15/2026
INVOICE N0. A-1098
focus: OCR line breaks, common O/0 error, and exact values

text: Please trans fer the docu ments to Jesslca before 5 PM.
focus: Recover OCR word breaks and likely Jessica typo conservatively

text: The office will be closed for Thanksgiving, but the warehouse will reopen Friday.
focus: Cultural holiday and reopening date

text: Bên em nghỉ từ 28 Tết đến mùng 6.
focus: Vietnamese lunar-calendar dates without false Gregorian dates

text: Dạ, em đã nhận được rồi ạ. Em cảm ơn anh.
focus: Politeness and natural English without literal particles

context: [Boss] The server failed for the third time today.
text: Great, exactly what we needed.
focus: Preserve sarcasm rather than positive approval

text: Please update the landing page CTA, nhưng đừng deploy trước khi tôi duyệt.
focus: Mixed English/Vietnamese and deployment prohibition

text: Rotate the API key, invalidate all active sessions, and do not log the token.
focus: Security terminology and three distinct actions

text: The tenant may terminate the agreement, but must give 30 days' written notice.
focus: May versus must and written-notice requirement

text: Ừ, để mai tính tiếp 😅 chứ giờ hết cứu rồi.
focus: Casual resigned tone and emoji
